element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns acceleration, braking, steering against 14 AI racers
Default attack; player learns directional variants (backhand, kick)
Combat depletes stamina; ejection from bike occurs on depletion
Boulders, cars, deer, cows cause crashes and stamina loss
Bottom-right corner display; color change (green to red) indicates depletion
Taken from opponents during combat; must disarm opponent while they hold weapon
Decreases on crashes; full depletion ends race and incurs repair costs
Starting $1,000; earned via race wins; used for bike purchases
Shop menu; different bikes have varying weight, speed, steering stats
Sporadic appearances; apprehension deducts fines; frequency increases per level
Available on certain bikes; double-tap acceleration for speed burst
Courses become longer; challenges endurance and pacing strategy
Opponents become more aggressive with each level; increases combat frequency
Repair bills and police fines increase per level; game-over if insufficient funds

typical_relief_triggers: Winning race at end of track
Opponent ejected from bike via stamina depletion
typical_tension_triggers: Low cash reserves approaching insufficient funds threshold
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Repetitive five-race cycles per level with escalating difficulty; combat encounters interspersed with racing stretches; tension buildup across level progression (longer tracks, aggressive AI, higher financial stakes)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Level 1 (Hawaii): Introduction to all core systems; manageable opponent difficulty; low financial risk; learning phase for combat mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 2-4 (Arizona, Tennessee, Alaska): Track length increases; AI aggression escalates; repair costs and fines rise; player must balance combat engagement with bike preservation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Level 5 (Vermont): Most aggressive opponents; longest tracks; highest financial penalties; police appear most frequently; requires mastery of all systems
notable_spikes: Level 2 transition: First significant difficulty jump with longer tracks and aggression increase
Level 5 finale: Cumulative challenge with all systems at maximum difficulty; financial failure risk highest

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 5 levels contains 5 distinct races across US locations (Hawaii, Arizona, Tennessee, Alaska, Vermont); progression requires winning 3rd place or higher on all tracks to advance
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 contains 5 races; player must win all 5 to unlock Level 2

notable_level_patterns.description: Repair bills, police fines, and prize money increase per level; creates mounting financial pressure requiring strategic bike purchases and race win consistency
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 has low penalties; Level 5 has high penalties, increasing game-over risk

notable_level_patterns.description: Races are won through placement finish, but combat with opponents is mandatory for strategic advantage; stamina depletion from fighting creates ejection risk requiring recovery runs
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels employ same pattern; complexity increases with AI aggression per level
